msContents.summary: Edited by Clark, The English Register of Godstow Nunnery.

msItem.title: Articles of excommunication
msItem.rubric: In constitucione Octobonis, capitulo Quum in armis.
msItem.incipit: Alle þat helpeth þe sareȝens aȝens cristen
msItem.explicit: as þis candele us qwenched and departed fro liȝt, and þei for to be departed fro god þat is liȝt
msItem.note: The surviving text preserves only the closing section of the Sarum sentence.
The text presents ten articles of excommunication, including prohibitions on aid to Saracens, witchcraft, illicit liturgical observance during interdict, and other disciplinary matters.
Related versions occur in Dulwich College 22, Durham University Library Cosin V.IV.2, MS Bodley 123, and Harley 2383.
msItem.textLang: Middle English with a Latin explicit

msItem.title: Verse translations of Latin liturgical texts
Invocation to the Cross
msItem.rubric: Here begynneth þe A.B.C. of deuocion
msItem.incipit:
✠ Of ihesu criste be euer oure spede
And kepe vs from perel of synnes and payne
msItem.explicit:
Titulle of þi passion Poynt us saue
As to thy ✠ reuerence we may haue
msItem.note: Four verses, each of seven lines.

msItem.title: Invocation of the Trinity
msItem.rubric: In nomine patris & filij et spiritus sancti. Amen.
msItem.incipit:
In the name of þe blessid trinyte
The fader þe sone and þe holi goost
msItem.explicit:
Thorgh vertu and grace of þis [cross] holy syne
Where on þow suffred þi passyon pyne
msItem.note: One stanza in rhyme royal.

msItem.title: Lord’s prayer
msItem.rubric: Pater noster qui es in celis sanctificetur nomen tuum
msItem.incipit:
oure fader in heuen halowed be þi name
As ihesus þis sone tawȝt us to say
msItem.explicit:
Raunsom us fader with mercy and garce
And bryng us al to þi blisful place. Amen.
msItem.note: Seven stanzas in Middle English verse following a Latin opening line.

msItem.title: Hail Mary
msItem.rubric: Aue maria gracia plena dominus tecum benedicta tu in mulieribus et cetera
msItem.incipit:
Hayle mary virgyn so ful of grace
Oure lorde almyghty is fully with þe
msItem.explicit:
Ihesus oure lorde god is sone and þyne
Pray hym for us blessid lady myne
msItem.note: One verse of seven lines.

msItem.title: Apostles’ Creed
msItem.rubric: Credo in deum patrem et cetera
msItem.incipit:
I beleue on god in persones þre
The fader and sone and holi gost
msItem.explicit:
Helpyng oure lyfe her to amende
And graunt us þe life þat neuer ende
msItem.note: Six verses, each of seven lines.

msItem.rubric: Confiteor deo et cetera
msItem.incipit:
I knowlech to god with veray contricoun
Vn to seynt mary and his seyntis alle
msItem.explicit:
With such vnderstondyng and clere wisdome
That y may come to þi kyngdome
msItem.note: Eleven verses, each of seven lines.

msItem.title: Confession to the saints and request for their prayers
msItem.rubric: Precor sanctam mariam omnes sanctos et cetera
msItem.incipit:
I pray þe lady þe moder of crist
Praieth ȝoure sone me for to spare
msItem.explicit:
Graunt me of ȝoure merites a participacion
And praieth oure lorde for me saluacyon
msItem.note: One stanza in rhyme royal.

msItem.title: Prayer for mercy
msItem.rubric: Misereatur mei omnipotens deus et cetera
msItem.incipit:
Now god almyghty haue mercy on me
ffor maryes prayers and al þi sayntes
msItem.explicit:
Wherfor of al my synnes mercy I cry
And pray the to bryng me to heuen an hy
msItem.note: One stanza in rhyme royal.

msItem.title: Commendation of the soul
msItem.rubric: In manus tuas et cetera
msItem.incipit:
In to þi handes lorde I take my soule
Whom þou boughtist with þi bittur passion
msItem.explicit:
Saue me fro payne and fro þe fende
And bring me to blis þat hath none ende
msItem.note: One stanza in rhyme royal; also found in MS. Douce 229.

msItem.title: Grace before and after meals
msItem.rubric: Pro gracijs Agendis
msItem.incipit:
Affore mete and aftir gracias say we
Thankyng þe lord of al þi grete grace
msItem.explicit:
Thyne almes [.]s redy to riche and pore
Euer blessid þou be good lorde þerfore
msItem.note: One stanza in rhyme royal.

msItem.title: Prayer for benefactors
msItem.rubric: Retribuere dignare et cetera
msItem.incipit:
Lorde þat ȝeuest us many þinges
Rewarde to al oure benefactours
msItem.explicit:
And sey gracias þere byfore þe
Amen lorde amen so mote hit be
msItem.note: Two stanzas in rhyme royal.

msItem.author: John Lydgate
msItem.title: Kalendere
msItem.incipit:
O ihesu lorde for þi Circumsicyon
In þe begynnyng as of þe new ȝere
msItem.explicit:
Now saue us fader with oure flessh þy worde
ffor Seynt Siluester loue at oure laste ende
msItem.note: Verse liturgical calendar, predominantly of English saints with additional papal and continental feasts; each month begins on a new leaf, with average verse length of seven lines; month names and saint names are rubricated.
msItem.textLang: Middle English with Latin month names

msItem.title: Cartulary of the nuns of Godstow Abbey, near Oxford
msItem.note: English abstract of a general cartulary, 1404, London, The National Archives, E 164/20 (edited by Amt).

msItem.title: Prologue to Godstow charters
msItem.incipit:
The prologe of the englyssh register.
The wyseman tawht hys child gladly to rede bokys
msItem.explicit: In the worshyp of oure lady and seynt John Baptist patron of thys seyd monastery the sentence for the more pertye of her mynymentys conteynyd in the boke of her reyster in latyn aftyr the same forme and ordyr of the seyd boke hath purposyd with goddys grace to make aftur hys conceyt fro latyn in to englyssh sentencyosly as foloweth thys simple translacion.
msItem.note: 23 lines. Middle English prologue explaining the rationale for translating the Latin register for practical use within the house.

msItem.title: History of the foundation of Godstow Abbey
msItem.incipit: The cronicle of the hows and monastery of Godstow makyth mensyon how that place ware fowndyde fyrst by reuelacyn in thys wyse in Wunchestre. In Wynchester was a lady bore of the worthiest blood
msItem.explicit: byn they to god commendyd that was of þe modyr bor that for us synners wolde fowchesafe to dye. he us graunt yf hym plesyde to his Joy to come. Amen.
msItem.note: An account of Dame Edifa of Winchester and the revelation to found Godstow.

msItem.title: Charters arranged by place
msItem.note: Charters are in approximate alphabetical order and grouped broadly by geographical proximity to Godstow.
Charter begin.

rubric.placeName: Williammsmore, del the grone, sthoretogrone, kyngsclere
Seynt Mighels parish atte South yate

note.placeName: Saint Michael's parish at the South Gate

rubric.placeName: Seynt Petres parissh in the Est

note.placeName: Saint Peter's parish in the East

msItem.rubric: Charters of kyng henry fitz Emperice
A confirmacion of kyng henry fitz Emperesse
A charter of kyng henry fitz emperice
A confirmacion of kyng henry fitz Emperice
Charters of kyng Richard kyng John kyng Henry the .iij.de
A Charter of kyng henry the iij.de
A confirmacion and a lycence of kyng henry the son of kyng John
A licence of kyng Edward to purchase and confirmacion
A licence of kyng Edward to purchase
A licence and confirmacion of kyng Edward the thirde
A confirmacion of pope Celestyne
A confirmacion of pope Eugenye
A bulle of pope Bonyface to take to farme ther tythes

msItem.title: Letter to the bishop of Worcester
msItem.note: Letter from Isabelle Braynton, nun of Godstow, dated 1509, to Silvester, bishop of Worcester.

msItem.title: Accounts of land rent
msItem.note: Accounts of land rent in 1496.

supportDesc.collation: 16–2 210 38 48 58–12nd leaf missing 68 78–1 88 96 108 118 128 136 148 158 168 178 188 198 208 218–12nd leaf missing 228–1 238 24Quire structure unclear 258 268 278–1 28Quire structure unclear 2910–2

layoutDesc.layout: Articles of excommunication in one column, ruled, with 35–40 lines per page; written space 140 154 .
Devotional poems in one column, ruled, with line division between verses; 38–40 lines per page; written space 240 85 .
Calendar text in one column with elaborate ruling; months in red at head; 32–35 lines per page; written space 240 150 .
Cartulary text in one ruled column with 42–43 lines per page; written space 260 155 .

handDesc.summary: Several scribes; elaborate ruling in the liturgical calendar; red and blue paraphs and headings; punctuated by low and medial points; running titles throughout.
handDesc.handNote: Devotional poetry in anglicana by a different scribe.
Bastard secretary appears in parts of the volume.

decoDesc.decoNote: Blue flourished initials with red penwork in devotional sections and calendar; in the calendar, the first line of each verse is marked with a blue initial.
Large blue decorated initials mark charter openings in the first two quires.

origin.origDate: after 1467, extended to 1480?, with later additions

history.provenance: Godstow, Oxfordshire, Abbey of St Mary the Virgin ad St John the Baptist, of Benedictine nuns. The prologue indicates that it is the work of a pore brodur and welwyller to the goode Abbas of Godstowe, Dame Alice Henley, and to all hyr couent, the whych byn for the more party in Englyssh bokys well y-lernyd, […] the sentence for the more partye of her munymentys conteynyd in the boke of her regystr’ in latyn, hath purposyd with goddys grace to make, […] fro latyn in-to englyssh (fol. 13r). Alice Henley was abbess 1446–70.
James Ware, 1594-1666: in his catalogue (1648) as Hist. MS. 16.
James Brydges, duke of Chandos, 1674-1744: sale 12 May 1746/1747, lot. 3125.

recordHist.source: Description by Mary O’Connor (2021), revised by Andrew Dunning (2026). Previously described in the Quarto Catalogue (W. D. Macray, Catalogi codicum manuscriptorum Bibliothecæ Bodleianæ...viri munificentissimi Ricardi Rawlinson, J.C.D., codicum...complectens, Quarto Catalogues V, 5 fascicles, 1862–1900). Summary Catalogue, Vol. 3, p. 201 Quarto Catalogue, Cols. 665–6

surrogates.bibl: Thompson, E. M., et al., eds, Facsimiles of Ancient Manuscripts, etc., First Series, 2 vols, New Palaeographical Society (London, 1912), pl. 196.
Wright, C. E., English Vernacular Hands from the Twelfth to the Fifteenth Centuries, Oxford Palaeographical Handbooks (Oxford, 1960), pl. 22.

listBibl.bibl: Amt, Emilie, ed., The Latin Cartulary of Godstow Abbey, Records of Social and Economic History, new ser., 52 (Oxford, 2014).
Clark, Andrew, ed., The English Register of Godstow Nunnery, near Oxford, Written about 1450, 3 vols, Early English Text Society, Original Series, 129, 130, 142 (London, 1905).
Davis, G. R. C., Medieval Cartularies of Great Britain and Ireland, 2nd edn, ed. Claire Breay, Julian Harrison, and David M. Smith (London, 2010), no. 463.

ref.title: MLGB3: Medieval Libraries of Great Britain

revisionDesc.change: Toby Burrows/Mapping Manuscript MigrationsProvenance and acquisition information added using https://github.com/mapping-manuscript-migrations/oxford-tei-updates/blob/master/update_oxford_prov.rb in collaboration with the Mapping Manuscript Migrations project.
James Cummings Up-converted the markup using https://github.com/jamescummings/Bodleian-msDesc-ODD/blob/master/convertTolkien2Bodley.xsl
